key_insight: MOSAIC shows that an orchestrated multi-LLM system can synthesize EHR evidence into nuanced disease-severity phenotypes that go beyond fixed rule-based scoring.
clinical_relevance: If validated more broadly, this approach could support more complete severity stratification from routine EHR data and help align risk assessment with outcomes.
method: Two-phase agentic LLM framework for severity phenotyping
Tested in type 2 diabetes using SyntheticMass synthetic EHR data
Compared against three algorithmic severity scores plus mortality and complication outcomes
evidence_signal: Open-weight MOSAIC closely matched the proprietary pipeline (weighted kappa 0.773) and showed moderate-to-fair agreement with existing scores, while agent-based tiers separated mortality and complications with log-rank p < 0.001.
watch_item: Results were shown in synthetic data and T2D, so real-world generalizability, bias, and safe deployment need further validation.
paper_title: Multi-Large Language Model Orchestrated Severity Assessment of Clinical Records (MOSAIC)
